anthropic-sandbox-runtime.caveat: No persistent lease, remote box, or SSH access

agent-sandbox.caveat: Requires kubectl, Agent Sandbox v0.5.0rc1 v1beta1 CRDs, a warm pool, explicit context, and RBAC

apple-container.bestFit: Local Linux containers on Apple silicon
apple-container.caveat: Requires Apple's container CLI and macOS

apple-machine.caveat: Delegated execution, not a normal SSH lease

apple-vm.bestFit: Headless Linux ARM64 VM on Apple silicon

ascii-box.caveat: Requires the ASCII Box CLI and account
ascii-box.auth: API key via ASCII_BOX_API_KEY or CRABBOX_ASCII_BOX_API_KEY; Box CLI config; direct remote SSH lease

aws.substrate: EC2 VM or dedicated Mac host
aws.bestFit: Broad Linux, Windows, WSL2, and macOS cloud coverage
aws.caveat: Largest configuration, quota, and cost surface
aws.auth: Broker-owned credentials through coordinator, or AWS SDK credential chain in direct mode; AWS_PROFILE AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY AWS_SESSION_TOKEN

aws-lambda-microvm.caveat: Requires a compatible Crabbox runner image; launch Regions and lifetime are limited
aws-lambda-microvm.auth: AWS SDK credential chain plus short-lived MicroVM auth token; delegated remote sandbox

azure.bestFit: Linux or Windows workloads in Azure
azure.caveat: Shared resource and identity setup is substantial
azure.auth: Broker-owned credentials through coordinator, or Azure DefaultAzureCredential, az login, service principal env credentials

azure-dynamic-sessions.bestFit: Short delegated container sessions in Azure
azure-dynamic-sessions.auth: Bearer token via CRABBOX_AZURE_DYNAMIC_SESSIONS_TOKEN or az account get-access-token; delegated remote sandbox

blaxel.caveat: Requires Blaxel API credentials and workspace access
blaxel.auth: API key via CRABBOX_BLAXEL_API_KEY or BL_API_KEY; env-only remote sandbox auth

blacksmith-testbox.bestFit: CI reproduction with proof and reusable sessions
blacksmith-testbox.caveat: Execution and artifacts follow the Testbox contract

cloudflare.caveat: Requires Worker deployment and container availability
cloudflare.auth: Runner bearer token via CRABBOX_CLOUDFLARE_RUNNER_TOKEN plus runner URL; delegated remote container

cloudflare-dynamic-workers.cleanup: terminal metadata and local claim removal
cloudflare-dynamic-workers.caveat: No shell, SSH, or filesystem sync; Dynamic Workers must be enabled
cloudflare-dynamic-workers.auth: Loader bearer token via CRABBOX_CLOUDFLARE_DYNAMIC_WORKERS_TOKEN; Wrangler or CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN for deployment

cloudflare-sandbox.bestFit: Cloudflare Sandbox Linux command execution through a bridge
cloudflare-sandbox.caveat: Requires a configured bridge URL; no SSH, browser, Tailscale, URL sessions, mounts, or checkpoints
cloudflare-sandbox.auth: Bridge bearer token via CRABBOX_CLOUDFLARE_SANDBOX_TOKEN plus bridge URL; delegated remote sandbox

codesandbox.caveat: Requires env-only SDK auth and a local Node bridge
codesandbox.auth: API key via CRABBOX_CODESANDBOX_API_KEY or CodeSandbox env auth; env-only remote sandbox auth

cua.bestFit: Experimental CUA diagnostics and existing-sandbox inspection
cua.caveat: Experimental and read-only; all remote lifecycle mutations fail closed until upstream exposes safe creation and deletion ownership primitives

coder.bestFit: Coder-backed Linux workspace over SSH proxy
coder.caveat: Requires the coder CLI, login, template access, and workspace quota

crownest.bestFit: Hosted agent-ready Workspace Runs with archive sync and durable evidence
crownest.caveat: No SSH, env forwarding, artifacts, proof, downloads, or custom Dockerfile templates yet
crownest.auth: API key via CRABBOX_CROWNEST_API_KEY or CROWNEST_API_KEY; bearer token remote sandbox auth

cubesandbox.caveat: Requires a CubeSandbox deployment, ready template ID, and reachable CubeProxy data plane
cubesandbox.auth: API key via CUBE_API_KEY plus Cube API endpoint; self-hosted remote delegated sandbox

daytona.bestFit: Managed development sandbox with direct toolbox execution or brokered SSH
daytona.caveat: SSH access is short-lived; direct run uses Daytona toolbox APIs while brokered run uses SSH
daytona.auth: Broker-owned API key through coordinator, or DAYTONA_API_KEY / DAYTONA_JWT_TOKEN in direct mode

digitalocean.auth: API token via DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN; direct remote Linux Droplet provider

docker-sandbox.bestFit: Local delegated sandbox with reusable session handles

e2b.caveat: URL bridge is provider-specific; no normal SSH lease
e2b.auth: API key via E2B_API_KEY; delegated remote sandbox auth

exe-dev.bestFit: Fast managed Linux VM exposed over SSH
exe-dev.caveat: Public SSH only; provider CLI owns auth

external.caveat: Safety and semantics depend on the configured executable

fastapi-cloud.bestFit: Inspecting FastAPI Cloud app deployment readiness
fastapi-cloud.caveat: Cannot execute arbitrary Crabbox run commands or stop apps
fastapi-cloud.auth: Bearer token via FASTAPI_CLOUD_TOKEN; service-control auth

firecracker.bestFit: Self-hosted Linux KVM host with prepared Firecracker kernel, rootfs, and CNI
firecracker.caveat: Requires Linux, /dev/kvm, Firecracker assets, and a working CNI setup on the host

freestyle.auth: API key via FREESTYLE_API_KEY; delegated remote VM auth

gcp.bestFit: Linux compute with broad machine selection
gcp.caveat: Project, IAM, quota, and firewall setup required
gcp.auth: Broker-owned credentials through coordinator, or Google ADC / gcloud / GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS in direct mode

hetzner.caveat: Linux-only and capacity varies by location
hetzner.auth: Broker-owned API token through coordinator, or HCLOUD_TOKEN in direct mode

hostinger.bestFit: Direct Linux VPS with persistent subscription
hostinger.caveat: Purchase needs opt-in and release does not cancel billing
hostinger.auth: API token via CRABBOX_HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN or HOSTINGER_API_TOKEN; direct remote VPS provider

islo.bestFit: Hosted delegated execution with keep, pause, and SSH helper
islo.caveat: SSH feature is not Crabbox-managed sync/run
islo.auth: API key via ISLO_API_KEY; provider-specific SSH helper and delegated remote auth

kubevirt.caveat: Needs KubeVirt, virtctl, and an SSH-ready template

lambda.bestFit: Direct GPU-backed Linux workload over SSH
lambda.caveat: Direct-only; billing, quota, and capacity are account-owned
lambda.auth: API key via LAMBDA_API_KEY; direct remote GPU cloud provider

linode.caveat: Direct-only; optional firewall must already exist
linode.auth: API token via LINODE_TOKEN; direct remote Linux instance provider

vultr.caveat: Direct-only; firewall groups and VPCs must already exist
vultr.auth: API key via VULTR_API_KEY; direct remote Linux instance provider

vast.cleanup: destroy by default; optional stop or keep
vast.bestFit: Direct Linux GPU lease from the Vast.ai offer market
vast.caveat: Direct-only and billable; capacity, quota, and offer availability vary
vast.auth: API key via CRABBOX_VAST_API_KEY or VAST_API_KEY; direct remote GPU instance provider

ovh.cleanup: instance, key, and local claim delete
ovh.caveat: Direct-only; credentials use OVH signed requests and local claims
ovh.auth: OVH signed request credentials via OVH_APPLICATION_KEY, OVH_APPLICATION_SECRET, OVH_CONSUMER_KEY; direct remote cloud provider

local-container.caveat: Isolation follows the local container runtime

modal.bestFit: Hosted Python or GPU-oriented delegated workloads
modal.caveat: Provider owns execution; no normal SSH lease
modal.auth: Modal token via MODAL_TOKEN_ID and MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET, or modal CLI login; delegated remote sandbox

morph.caveat: Release retains the paused instance unless deleteOnRelease is enabled
morph.auth: API key via MORPH_API_KEY or CRABBOX_MORPH_API_KEY; direct remote VM provider

mxc.caveat: Windows host and execution-container support required

nvidia-brev.bestFit: Managed NVIDIA GPU workspace over SSH
nvidia-brev.caveat: Requires Brev CLI auth, quota, and available GPU capacity

nebius.bestFit: Direct Linux VM lease with optional GPU selection
nebius.caveat: Requires Nebius CLI auth, project/subnet setup, quota, and public SSH
nebius.auth: Nebius CLI auth and project/subnet config; direct remote cloud provider; secrets stay in CLI store

namespace-devbox.bestFit: Fast managed development box over SSH
namespace-devbox.caveat: Uses the devbox product, not Namespace Compute instances

namespace-instance.bestFit: Short-lived managed Linux compute over SSH
namespace-instance.caveat: Requires the nsc CLI and direct provider credentials

nomad.bestFit: Self-hosted delegated Linux execution on an existing Nomad cluster
nomad.caveat: Requires Nomad HTTP API access, allocation exec privileges, and an env-only token when ACLs are enabled
nomad.auth: Nomad HTTP API token via NOMAD_TOKEN when ACLs are enabled; self-hosted delegated remote execution

opencomputer.caveat: REST execution contract, not an SSH lease
opencomputer.auth: API key via CRABBOX_OPENCOMPUTER_API_KEY, OPENCOMPUTER_API_KEY, or oc CLI config; sent only as X-API-Key

opensandbox.bestFit: Hosted delegated sandbox through an open SDK
opensandbox.caveat: Requires compatible OpenSandbox control and exec endpoints
opensandbox.auth: API key via CRABBOX_OPENSANDBOX_API_KEY plus CRABBOX_OPENSANDBOX_API_URL; remote sandbox auth

orgo.bestFit: Managed Linux computer execution through an HTTP API
orgo.caveat: No normal SSH lease or Crabbox workspace sync
orgo.auth: API key via CRABBOX_ORGO_API_KEY or ORGO_API_KEY; delegated remote computer auth

parallels.bestFit: Local macOS, Linux, or Windows VM with snapshots
parallels.caveat: Requires prepared Parallels source VMs and SSH

phala.substrate: Phala Cloud confidential Intel TDX CVM
phala.bestFit: Short-lived confidential Linux compute over SSH
phala.caveat: Requires the phala CLI and its stored auth; verifies Intel TDX attestation by default (needs outbound Intel PCS network; --phala-skip-attestation to opt out)
phala.auth: Phala CLI auth or PHALA_CLOUD_API_KEY; direct remote confidential VM provider

proxmox.caveat: Needs a prepared template, guest agent, and network

railway.bestFit: Inspecting or stopping an existing Railway service
railway.caveat: Cannot execute arbitrary Crabbox run commands
railway.auth: API token via RAILWAY_API_TOKEN; service-control auth

runpod.bestFit: GPU-backed Linux workload over public SSH
runpod.caveat: Capacity, GPU pricing, and public SSH vary
runpod.auth: API key via RUNPOD_API_KEY; direct remote GPU pod provider

scaleway.bestFit: Direct Linux VM on Scaleway Instances
scaleway.caveat: Direct-only; security groups must already allow SSH
scaleway.auth: Scaleway SDK credentials and project/zone config; direct remote cloud provider

semaphore.bestFit: Debugging in the same image and secret plane as CI
semaphore.caveat: Depends on debug SSH metadata from the job
semaphore.auth: API token via CRABBOX_SEMAPHORE_TOKEN or SEMAPHORE_API_TOKEN; CI remote proof runner

sealos-devbox.bestFit: Sealos DevBox Linux workspace over SSHGate or NodePort
sealos-devbox.caveat: Requires kubectl, explicit context, DevBox CRD/RBAC, image, and SSHGate or NodePort route configuration

smolvm.auth: API key via SMOLMACHINES_API_KEY, CRABBOX_SMOLVM_API_KEY, or SMK_API_KEY; delegated remote microVM auth

sprites.bestFit: Fast Linux microVM over provider SSH proxy
sprites.caveat: SSH transport depends on sprite proxy
sprites.auth: API token via CRABBOX_SPRITES_TOKEN, SPRITES_TOKEN, SPRITE_TOKEN, or SETUP_SPRITE_TOKEN; direct remote microVM provider

ssh.bestFit: Bring-your-own persistent Linux, macOS, or Windows host
ssh.caveat: Crabbox does not provision or clean up the host

superserve.caveat: Requires both control-plane and data-plane access
superserve.auth: API key via CRABBOX_SUPERSERVE_API_KEY or SUPERSERVE_API_KEY; delegated remote sandbox auth

vercel-sandbox.caveat: Requires SDK bridge support and Vercel Sandbox auth
vercel-sandbox.auth: OIDC or access token via VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN, VERCEL_TOKEN, VERCEL_AUTH_TOKEN, or CRABBOX_VERCEL_SANDBOX_* vars; delegated remote sandbox

tart.caveat: Apple silicon host and prepared Tart image required

tencentcloud.bestFit: Linux SSH leases on Tencent Cloud CVM
tencentcloud.caveat: Direct-only; requires CVM image, VPC/subnet/security-group planning, and Tencent Cloud API credentials
tencentcloud.auth: Tencent Cloud API credentials; direct remote CVM provider

tenki.bestFit: Managed Linux sandbox with SSH proxy
tenki.caveat: Gateway auth uses Tenki-managed key and certificate files
tenki.auth: Tenki CLI browser auth and Tenki-managed SSH key/cert files; managed remote sandbox provider

tensorlake.caveat: Does not expose raw Firecracker provisioning
tensorlake.auth: API key via TENSORLAKE_API_KEY; delegated remote Firecracker sandbox auth

unikraft-cloud.lifecycle: Crabbox claim plus Unikraft Cloud instance
unikraft-cloud.bestFit: Running a prebuilt OCI image as a cloud microVM service
unikraft-cloud.caveat: No SSH, workspace sync, or arbitrary command execution; package the app as an OCI image first
unikraft-cloud.auth: API key via CRABBOX_UNIKRAFT_CLOUD_API_KEY, UNIKRAFT_CLOUD_API_KEY, UKC_API_KEY, or UKC_TOKEN; remote service-control auth

upstash-box.caveat: No normal SSH access or coordinator routing
upstash-box.auth: API key via UPSTASH_BOX_API_KEY or CRABBOX_UPSTASH_BOX_API_KEY; delegated remote sandbox auth

wandb.bestFit: Delegated ML or GPU run environment
wandb.caveat: Execution follows the W&B run contract
wandb.auth: API key via CRABBOX_WANDB_API_KEY or WANDB_API_KEY; delegated remote GPU/ML run auth

windows-sandbox.caveat: Requires Windows Sandbox and local host automation

xcp-ng.bestFit: Self-hosted Linux VM pool over XAPI
xcp-ng.caveat: Normal leases require prepared Linux templates
